name: /assistant on iPad portrait — three-column shell collapses cleanly
description: On 1024×1366 portrait (iPad Pro), the standard three-column /assistant layout (sidebar / threads / chat) doesn't fit. The threads column should collapse to a drawer triggered by a button in the chat header; the sidebar stays as a thin rail; the chat composer is touch-friendly.

persona.name: Diego Martínez
persona.context: Holds the iPad in portrait while talking to a customer. Wants to glance at his thread list when needed.

steps.expect: Sidebar is in narrow rail mode (icons only); threads drawer is closed; chat takes the full remaining width

steps.action: Taps the threads-drawer toggle in the chat header
steps.expect: Drawer slides in from the left, overlaying the chat; tapping outside closes it
Drawer auto-closes; chat updates to the picked thread
Three-column layout returns; drawer state resets

evaluationCriteria.check: Sidebar rail icons retain hover states; chat composer doesn't shrink below 14px font
Tablet behavior reads as 'designed-for', not 'desktop squeezed onto tablet'
Orientation change is responsive (matchMedia listener), not a full reload
Drawer toggle has aria-expanded; focus traps inside drawer when open and returns on close
